must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume mentions 'Team leadership and training' at Ventra Health, but in a Facilities context, not software engineering.
Role involves vendor coordination and client approvals, suggesting communication capability, but not in a technical SW domain.

reasoning: Reject — completely wrong domain. This candidate is a Facilities and Transportation management professional with nine years of experience managing cab fleets, vendor contracts, and office administration. There is zero evidence of C programming, MISRA, AUTOSAR, RTOS, ADAS, or RADAR anywhere in this resume. The educational background is B.Com Computers — not an engineering degree. This resume has been submitted in error or through a severely mismatched application. Do not spend further recruiter time on this profile; reject immediately and move to the next candidate.
strengths: TEAM LEADERSHIP IN OPERATIONS: Demonstrated multi-site facilities and transport leadership at Ventra Health managing vendors, fleet compliance, GPS monitoring, and manpower — credible operational leadership, but entirely irrelevant to embedded SW development.

END-TO-END MIS & REPORTING: Consistent evidence of building and maintaining MIS dashboards, KPI reports, OTA/OTD summaries, and billing audits across multiple employers — a transferable analytical habit, but not the signal this JD needs.

VENDOR & CONTRACT MANAGEMENT: Managed fleet vendors, driver compliance, and EHS audits across Cognizant (4,000 employees), RouteMatic, and Invesco — scale of operations is notable, but irrelevant to C/AUTOSAR/RADAR development.
gaps: ZERO EMBEDDED SOFTWARE SKILLS: The JD mandates C programming, MISRA guidelines, AUTOSAR, and RTOS as hard requirements. This candidate has no software development background of any kind — not a gap that can be bridged; it is a domain mismatch.

WRONG EDUCATIONAL AND PROFESSIONAL DOMAIN: A B.Com Computers degree and nine years in Facilities and Transport management disqualify this candidate from a Senior Embedded SW Developer role requiring RADAR/ADAS expertise — this is a fundamental career domain mismatch.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated and latest role end-date is March 2026 — no penalty applied as information is indeterminate.
Candidate location is not explicitly stated in the resume, so no location penalty can be confirmed.
Candidate has nine years of experience, exceeding the four-year minimum threshold, so no penalty applies — though the experience is entirely in the wrong domain.
JD gender preference is 'Any', so no penalty applies.
No college preference or exclusion specified in the JD, so no adjustment applies.

hard_filter_failures: Wrong domain — zero technical skill match for an embedded SW Developer role
availability_reason: Latest role end-date listed as March 2026, indicating the candidate is currently between roles.
self_reported_unverified: Claims to manage transport for 4,000 employees 24x7 at Cognizant — scale is impressive but no supporting metrics or headcount data are provided.
End-date of 'March 2026' at Ventra Health is listed as a future date relative to most evaluation windows — raises question of whether role is active or ended.
interview_flags: Confirm why this resume was submitted for an Embedded C/AUTOSAR SW Developer role — likely a misapplication or recruiter database error.
Probe whether the candidate has any unreported software engineering background or side projects in C/embedded systems.
Clarify employment status — role end-date of March 2026 at Ventra Health appears to be in the future or very recent; confirm if currently employed.
Verify the B.Com Computers degree — assess if any coursework covered C programming or embedded systems fundamentals.
Ask if candidate intended to apply for a Facilities or Operations Manager role instead — redirect appropriately.
